problem_addressed: Existing semi-supervised learning methods rely on thresholded pseudo labeling but fail to incorporate uncertainty from noisy labels or out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations.
core_claim: Complementing SSL with an uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty improves performance without adding computational overhead.
key_contributions: Novel uncertainty-aware objective incorporating aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty to align uncertainty distributions of labeled and unlabeled domains
Computationally lightweight framework that avoids label propagation graphs and similarity computations
State-of-the-art results on complex datasets like CIFAR-100 (79.32% accuracy) and Mini-ImageNet
novelty: First effort to estimate and align uncertainty distributions of labeled and unlabeled data in SSL, unlike prior work focused only on consistency regularization
method_summary: Proposes an unsupervised uncertainty-aware loss function that quantifies aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty during pseudo labeling, complementing existing SSL methods without requiring ensembles or graph constructions.
method_components: Pseudo labeling mechanism using weak/strong augmentations with confidence thresholding
Uncertainty quantification for both aleatoric (data) and epistemic (model) uncertainty
Loss function that applies only to high-confidence pseudo labels (max(fθ(xi)) > τc)
key_innovation: Aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains to handle noisy pseudo labels and out-of-distribution samples efficiently
assumptions: Unlabeled data shares similar distribution with labeled data
Uncertainty can be quantified efficiently without Bayesian methods
Aligning uncertainty distributions improves SSL generalization
theoretical_grounding: None provided; paper focuses on empirical evaluation over theoretical analysis
ablations: Exponential moving average for pseudo labels
improvement_over_baseline: 0.65% over SimPLE on CIFAR-10 (94.92% vs 94.27%), 0.03% over SimPLE on CIFAR-100 (97.50% vs 97.54%), 0.26% over SimPLE on Mini-ImageNet (50.75% vs 49.39%)
stated_limitations: Existing SSL methods do not incorporate uncertainty of pseudo-labels or unlabeled samples
Current approaches don't account for uncertainty due to noisy labels or out-of-distribution samples
Thresholded pseudo-labeling methods lack uncertainty consideration
unstated_weaknesses: Potential computational overhead from uncertainty quantification
May require additional hyperparameters for uncertainty estimation
Limited evaluation on diverse datasets might question generalizability
threats_to_validity: Distribution shift between labeled and unlabeled data might affect uncertainty estimates
Strong augmentations could introduce uncertainty not captured by the method
Potential overfitting to specific uncertainty patterns in training data
reproducibility: unknown (insufficient information about code availability and experimental details)
open_questions: What specific uncertainty quantification techniques are used for aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty?
How does the uncertainty-aware objective integrate with existing SSL frameworks?
What is the computational overhead of the proposed method?
How does the method balance uncertainty with pseudo-label confidence?
future_work: Likely includes extending to more SSL scenarios or other uncertainty types
gaps_this_enables: Integration of uncertainty quantification into SSL pipelines
relevance_to_topic: This paper directly addresses uncertainty quantification in SSL, which is highly relevant to uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection.
limitations_stated: Existing SSL methods do not incorporate uncertainty of pseudo-labels or unlabeled samples
Current approaches don't account for uncertainty due to noisy labels or out-of-distribution samples
Thresholded pseudo-labeling methods lack uncertainty consideration
title: Complementing Semi-Supervised Learning with Uncertainty Quantification
